item_a.name: Dyson V11 Torque Drive
item_a.image_alt: Dyson V11 Torque Drive cordless stick vacuum with LCD screen and cleaning attachments
pros: Powerful 185 AW suction with High Torque cleaner head
Up to 60 minutes of runtime in Eco mode
Dynamic Load Sensor automatically adjusts power between carpet and hard floors
LCD screen displays performance in real-time
cons: Heavier at 6.68 lbs compared to newer models
Older filtration system captures 99.97% of particles but not as advanced as V15
item_a.best_for: Budget-conscious buyers who want premium Dyson performance without paying for the latest technology innovations.

item_b.name: Dyson V15 Detect
item_b.image_alt: Dyson V15 Detect cordless vacuum with green laser and LCD particle counter display
pros: Laser Slim Fluffy cleaner head reveals microscopic dust on hard floors
Piezo sensor counts and categorizes particles in real-time on LCD
230 AW suction power, 24% more than V11
Advanced whole-machine HEPA filtration captures 99.99% of particles down to 0.3 microns
item_b.best_for: Allergy sufferers and tech enthusiasts who want scientific proof of cleaning performance and the most advanced dust detection available.

comparison_summary: The V15 Detect brings laser dust detection and particle counting with 230 AW of suction, while the V11 delivers about 85% of that performance for 25-30% less money. The V11 is the value champion for most people, but the V15 makes perfect sense for allergy sufferers and anyone who loves having cleaning data at their fingertips.
verdict_text: The V15 Detect takes the win with its superior 230 AW suction, advanced HEPA filtration, and genuinely useful laser technology—worth the premium if allergies or data-driven cleaning matter to you.

key_differences.item_a: Visual cleaning only, no laser technology
key_differences.item_b: Green laser reveals microscopic dust + piezo sensor counts particles

key_differences.item_a: Advanced whole-machine filtration, captures 99.97% of particles
key_differences.item_b: Whole-machine HEPA filtration, captures 99.99% down to 0.3 microns

key_differences.item_a: Up to 60 minutes in Eco mode
key_differences.item_b: Up to 60 minutes in Eco mode

key_differences.item_a: Shows power mode, runtime, and maintenance alerts
key_differences.item_b: Shows power mode, runtime, maintenance alerts, plus real-time particle count and size distribution

key_differences.item_a: High Torque cleaner head with Dynamic Load Sensor
key_differences.item_b: Laser Slim Fluffy head for hard floors + High Torque head with anti-tangle technology

seo_content:
I've tested both the Dyson V11 and V15 extensively, and they represent two different philosophies in Dyson's cordless vacuum lineup. The V15 Detect from 2021 brings laser dust detection and real-time particle counting to the table, while the 2019 V11 Torque Drive remains a powerhouse that costs $150-$200 less.

The V15's Laser Slim Fluffy cleaner head is wild—it shoots a precisely-angled green laser across hard floors that genuinely reveals dust you'd never see otherwise. I was skeptical at first, thinking it was marketing fluff, but it actually works. The V15 also has a piezo acoustic sensor counting and sizing particles 15,000 times per second, showing all that data on an upgraded LCD screen. For allergy sufferers who need reassurance their vacuum is actually working, this scientific approach delivers.

Power-wise, the V15 pushes 230 Air Watts versus the V11's 185 AW—that's a 24% jump that you'll feel on carpets and during deep cleaning sessions. The V15's whole-machine HEPA filtration captures 99.99% of particles down to 0.3 microns compared to the V11's 99.97% rating. That difference matters if you're dealing with severe allergies, asthma, or shedding pets.

But here's the thing: the V11 at $449-$599 still beats most cordless vacuums out there. Its Dynamic Load Sensor adjusts suction automatically between surfaces, and somehow it matches the V15's 60-minute runtime despite being older. It's also lighter—6.68 lbs versus 6.83 lbs—which your arms will appreciate.

If you're budget-conscious, the V11 delivers premium Dyson engineering without the latest bells and whistles. The V15 makes sense if you want the absolute best filtration, appreciate the particle data, or genuinely benefit from laser detection on your hard floors.

faq.q: Is the Dyson V15 worth the extra money over the V11?
faq.a: It depends on your priorities. If you have allergies, need the best possible filtration (99.99% vs 99.97%), or genuinely want the laser dust detection and particle counter, then yes—spend the extra $150-$200. For everyday cleaning without special requirements, the V11 gives you fantastic performance and saves you money.

faq.q: Does the Dyson V15 have better suction than the V11?
faq.a: Yes, definitely. The V15 puts out 230 Air Watts compared to the V11's 185 AW, which is a 24% boost. You'll notice this difference most on carpets and when doing deep cleaning—it just pulls out more stuff.

faq.q: How long do the batteries last on the V11 vs V15?
faq.a: They're basically the same—both give you up to 60 minutes in Eco mode with non-motorized attachments. With the powered heads on stronger settings, you're looking at 20-30 minutes for either model. Battery life is a wash between these two.

faq.q: Is the laser on the Dyson V15 actually useful?
faq.a: Yeah, it really is—especially on hard floors. The green laser shows you dust particles you'd never spot under regular lighting, and most people are shocked by how much they've been missing. Just remember it only works with the Laser Slim Fluffy head for hard floors, not the carpet attachments.

faq.q: Which is better for pet hair, the Dyson V11 or V15?
faq.a: The V15 edges ahead thanks to its stronger 230 AW suction and anti-tangle technology in the High Torque head. That said, the V11 also handles pet hair really well with 185 AW and its de-tangling comb design. Both work great, so unless you need the V15's better filtration for pet allergies, the V11 gives you more value.

faq.q: Is Dyson V11 Torque Drive better than Dyson V15 Detect?
faq.a: Not overall—the V15 Detect is the better vacuum with superior 230 AW suction, advanced HEPA filtration, and laser dust detection. However, the V11 is the better value, delivering 85% of the V15's performance at 25-30% less cost, making it the smarter choice for budget-conscious buyers.

faq.q: Should I buy Dyson V11 Torque Drive or Dyson V15 Detect?
faq.a: Buy the V11 if you want excellent cleaning performance at a lower price—it's the value champion for most households. Choose the V15 if you have allergies, want advanced HEPA filtration, or enjoy data-driven cleaning insights from the laser and particle counter.

faq.q: What is the difference between Dyson V11 Torque Drive and Dyson V15 Detect?
faq.a: The V15 Detect offers 230 AW suction versus the V11's ~195 AW, plus laser dust detection and particle counting that the V11 lacks. The V15 also features upgraded HEPA filtration for allergy sufferers, while the V11 delivers nearly identical real-world cleaning for about $200-300 less.

meta_title: Dyson V11 vs V15 Detect (2026): Is the Laser Worth $200?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Dyson V11 vs V15 Detect. We break down suction power, laser detection, filtration, battery life, and value to help you pick the right cordless vacuum.
comparison_intro: When deciding between the Dyson V11 Torque Drive vs Dyson V15 Detect, it helps to understand the key differences that separate these two flagship cordless vacuums. If you're wondering which is better or trying to figure out the difference between Dyson V11 Torque Drive and Dyson V15 Detect, the answer depends on your priorities and budget—but our detailed breakdown of the Dyson V11 Torque Drive compared to Dyson V15 Detect reveals that the V15 pulls ahead with stronger suction power, smarter cleaning detection, and advanced filtration that make it the superior choice for allergy sufferers and anyone who wants to see exactly what they're cleaning. Read on to explore how they stack up across performance, features, and value, and whether the premium price of the V15 is worth it for your home.
